role_title: Staff Product Manager, Lyft AI Platform & Marketplace Applications

location.office_time_required: 3 days per week (Mondays, Wednesdays, Thursdays); flexibility to work from anywhere up to 4 weeks per year

level_signals: Works directly with senior leaders and CXO-level executives
Expected to operate independently with little supervision
Base pay range $176K-$220K in SF, consistent with Staff-level IC

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: Experience driving technically complex products end to end, from inception, to launch with significant user adoption, to sunset

Experience in managing (from product perspective) a team of software engineers and scientists, tracking the progress towards a roadmap through appropriate tools (Jira, Linear, Spreadsheets, Clickup etc)
Experience in crafting data visualizations for the automated measurements of KPIs and metrics, starting from raw databases (SQL, Tableau, Looker, Mode, Python visualization packages etc)
Familiarity with crafting and leading presentations to small groups at the CXO executives level and to big teams (100+ ppl) at an all hands like level in medium to large companies
Ease in driving results and operating independently and with little supervision in flat structured organizations where approvals are not needed but results are closely monitored
Strong understanding of agentic systems, context engineering, and multi-agent orchestration
Strong conceptual knowledge of machine learning and AI
Experience in handling third parties as vendors or customers and knowledge of Saas contract practices and software sales processes
Excellent skills in documenting and articulating complex technical systems.

nice_to_haves.raw_text_from_jd: hands-on coding experience with standard libraries (e.g., PyTorch, Keras, Scikit-learn, XGBoost) is a plus

scope.surface: Agentic AI product and platform; AI/ML platform powering hundreds of applications across Lyft; AI-powered marketplace products
scope.business_model: B2C ridesharing platform with internal AI/ML platform serving internal stakeholders
primary_metrics_inferred: AI platform adoption across the company
KPIs and metrics for AI/ML-powered marketplace products
core_responsibilities: Define, own and execute the roadmap of the AI platform and AI-powered marketplace products
Define, track and periodically report on metrics and KPIs of the org
Engage teams and users of AI platform inside the company to understand their needs, evangelize current offerings and identify gaps or opportunities
Write detailed product requirements, strategy and planning documents aimed at both technical and non technical audiences
Proactively engage, research and craft proposals for areas of the company where AI platform can drive efficiency and/or increase effectiveness
Present to non technical leadership on the progress of the team quarterly
Oversee and guide build vs buy decisions, grounding them in data and research

compensation.equity_form: potential equity offering mentioned but not specified

green_flags: Compensation range disclosed ($176K-$220K base in SF)
Clear product surface and ownership (Agentic AI platform + marketplace applications)
Role has high visibility — direct engagement with senior leaders and CXO-level executives
Hybrid policy is explicit (3 days/week Mon/Wed/Thu) with flexibility for 4 remote weeks per year
Generous benefits package including 18 weeks parental leave and 401(k) match
red_flags: San Francisco hybrid only (3 days/week in office) — eliminates fully-remote candidates
Broad scope combining AI platform ownership with marketplace applications may indicate thin PM coverage or resource constraints
Visa sponsorship status not mentioned — unclear for non-US candidates
The JD conflates must-haves and nice-to-haves in a single 'Experience' section, making hard/soft classification ambiguous for some items

fields_inferred_not_stated: seniority.level (inferred from role title 'Staff PM', comp range, and years requirement)
scope.primary_metrics_inferred (inferred from responsibilities; not explicitly stated)
location.visa_sponsorship (not mentioned in JD; marked unclear)
ambiguities: The entire 'Experience' section is presented as a flat list without 'Required' vs 'Preferred' labels. The ML coding hands-on experience is explicitly called out as 'a plus'; all other items are treated as hard requirements. This interpretation is noted.
Bonus is mentioned in the compensation disclosure ('not inclusive of potential equity offering, bonus or benefits') but no bonus range is provided.
Location pay differential is implied ('Salary ranges are dependent on... geographic location') but no other city ranges are stated.
